company.name: Valneva SE
company.subtitle: Post-VALOR Phase 3 · IXCHIQ US withdrawn · S4V2 Phase 2 pending

assets.name: VLA15/LB6V (Lyme) — royalties
assets.partner: Pfizer (commercializes; Valneva receives 14-22% tiered royalties + up to $243M milestones)

market.notes: €150M = Valneva peak annual royalties assuming Pfizer does ~€800M in Lyme sales at ~19% blended royalty. penPct=100 means Valneva gets its full royalty; penMult scales Pfizer launch speed.

us.patientsK.note: CDC: ~20M US in Lyme-endemic areas (Northeast/Midwest); ~476K new cases/yr; Pfizer/Valneva VLA15 P3 ongoing.

eu.patientsK.note: ECDC: ~15M EU-5 endemic exposure (Germany/Austria/Scandinavia); ~85K cases/yr reported.

row.patientsK.note: Global Lyme-endemic exposure ~50M; ROW mostly Canada/Russia/China endemic focus areas.

us.wtpPct.note: ~40% -- ACIP likely to recommend post-approval for endemic residents + outdoor workers; school-age focus.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~30% -- STIKO/JCVI likely endemic-region recommendation; similar TBE vaccine adoption pattern.

row.wtpPct.note: ~15% -- Canada NACI + Russia endemic; most ROW low priority for immunization programs.

us.priceK.note: VLA15 modeled ~$0.2K per dose (3-dose primary + boosters); Pfizer adult/pediatric pricing.

eu.priceK.note: ~$0.15K per dose post-HTA; national tender pricing likely lower.

row.priceK.note: ~$0.1K per dose blended; Canada/Russia primary markets.

penPct.note: ~25% peak of endemic eligible pool within 5yr; competition from Moderna mRNA Lyme (mRNA-1975).

us.reachPct.note: VLA15 6-valent Lyme with Pfizer (VALOR Ph3); ~500K US Lyme cases/yr, addressable ~8M at-risk endemic NE/Upper Midwest; launch 2026 ~25% reach.

us.priceK.note: ~$0.22K ($220) 3-dose primary series; anchor to Shingrix $360 + RSV $295; VLA15 royalty to Valneva 14-22% on Pfizer net sales.

us.wtpPct.note: ~70% -- CDC ACIP adult Lyme recommendation expected; commercial + Medicare Part D vaccine coverage post-recommendation; ACA preventive $0 copay.

eu.reachPct.note: ~30% EU5 Lyme-endemic zones (Germany, Austria, C. Europe highest); EMA filing 2026 post-VALOR; national immunization programs drive uptake.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~50% -- STIKO Germany likely to recommend; G-BA vaccine mandate post-recommendation; France HCSP under review.

row.reachPct.note: ~12% ROW; Japan PMDA Lyme low prevalence; China Lyme endemic NE provinces but vaccine access limited.

row.wtpPct.note: ~18% -- Japan NHI vaccine mature; China NIP limited Lyme coverage.

assets.name: IXCHIQ (Chikungunya)
assets.stage: US license suspended Aug 22, 2025; BLA+IND voluntarily withdrawn Jan 2026. Ex-US commercial (EU, Canada, UK, Brazil).

market.notes: US permanently lost. Ex-US includes EU/Canada/UK/Brazil + outbreak response. Competitor: Bavarian Nordic's Vimkunya (VLP-based, FDA approved Feb 2025). Ex-US contributed ~€7.5M in H1 2025.

us.patientsK.note: CDC: ~500K potential doses/yr US travel + endemic Puerto Rico/USVI; Ixchiq (Valneva) approved 2023 age 18+.

eu.patientsK.note: EMA approved Ixchiq 2024; ~200K EU-5 doses/yr travelers to Americas/SSA/South Asia endemic zones.

row.patientsK.note: WHO: global chikungunya outbreak-prone; ~3M doses/yr endemic (Brazil, India, SE Asia) + travel.

us.wtpPct.note: ~45% -- ACIP recommends for travelers to outbreak areas + lab workers; not routine immunization.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~35% -- EMA approved; national travel clinics + outbreak response; not in routine schedule.

row.wtpPct.note: ~20% -- Brazil ANVISA approved 2024; outbreak stockpile procurement (WHO/Gavi emerging).

us.priceK.note: Ixchiq ~$0.3K per dose list (single-dose live attenuated); travel clinic pricing.

eu.priceK.note: ~$0.2K per dose post-HTA; national immunization tenders lower.

row.priceK.note: ~$0.05K per dose tiered via PAHO/Gavi for endemic Brazil/LatAm; single-dose.

penPct.note: ~30% peak of addressable travel + outbreak response; competition from Bavarian Nordic VLA1553-bivalent.

us.reachPct.note: IXCHIQ single-dose chikungunya vaccine; first-in-class FDA approval Nov 2023; ACIP travel rec Feb 2024; ~8M US travelers to endemic areas; launch reach 20% yr3.

us.priceK.note: $0.30K ($305) WAC per dose; single dose; travel clinic + PBM channel.

us.wtpPct.note: ~60% -- ACIP travel recommendation drives commercial + VFC; FDA label 18+ (age pause 2024 for 65+ post-AE signal resolved).

eu.reachPct.note: ~18% EU5; EMA approved Jun 2024; French Ministry Reunion emergency use 2024 outbreak; Italy/Spain endemic surveillance growing.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~35% -- French HAS emergency use 2024; Italy/Spain/Portugal HTA travel vaccine channels.

row.reachPct.note: ~30% ROW; Brazil ANVISA 2024, India mass epidemic use potential; WHO PQ pending for GAVI.

row.wtpPct.note: ~35% -- Brazil SUS + India state programs post-outbreak; tiered pricing GAVI.

row.priceK.note: ~$0.08K ROW avg; GAVI tiered $3-10 for LMICs; Brazil $40 negotiated.

assets.name: S4V2 (Shigella)
assets.stage: Phase 2 (two trials ongoing: infant safety/immunogenicity + Phase 2b CHIM human challenge)
assets.partner: LimmaTech Biologics (sponsors Phase 2; Valneva takes over if positive)

market.notes: WHO priority. No approved multivalent Shigella vaccine outside Russia/China. Global market estimated >$500M/yr. Second leading cause of fatal diarrhea worldwide.

us.patientsK.note: CDC: ~5M global potential doses/yr travel + endemic (shigella pediatric LMIC, Zika outbreak response).

eu.patientsK.note: EMA/ECDC: travel clinic + outbreak response; shigella pediatric focus for LMIC via Gavi; Zika stockpile.

row.patientsK.note: WHO shigella LMIC pediatric ~100M birth cohort Gavi-eligible; Zika endemic Americas/APAC.

us.wtpPct.note: ~25% -- shigella primarily LMIC pediatric; US travel niche; Zika outbreak-response procurement.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~20% -- travel clinic + outbreak stockpile; EMA approval pathways open for both.

row.wtpPct.note: ~35% -- Gavi procurement for LMIC pediatric shigella; Zika PAHO stockpile LatAm.

us.priceK.note: Shigella/Zika vaccines modeled ~$0.15K per dose US travel; outbreak response procurement.

eu.priceK.note: ~$0.1K per dose travel clinic; lower via tender.

row.priceK.note: ~$0.02K per dose Gavi tiered (shigella LMIC); Zika outbreak stockpile similar.

penPct.note: ~20% peak -- early-stage assets; shigella strong LMIC volume + Gavi procurement potential.

us.reachPct.note: S4V2 tetravalent Shigella vaccine; Ph2 2024; ~450K US Shigella cases/yr but major target is travel + pediatric LMIC; launch 2028 ~15% US travel reach.

us.priceK.note: ~$0.18K per dose projected; anchor to travel vaccine class $150-350.

us.wtpPct.note: ~50% -- travel clinic + military; CDC ACIP travel rec pending; GAVI pediatric LMIC focus.

eu.reachPct.note: ~12% EU5 travel; EMA filing 2028+; ECDC Shigella surveillance low in EU resident pop.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~25% -- travel vaccine HTA via private channels.

row.reachPct.note: ~25% ROW; GAVI Shigella pediatric major market LMIC (Asia/Africa); WHO PQ gate.

row.wtpPct.note: ~30% -- GAVI tiered; national pediatric immunization programs post-WHO PQ.

market.notes: Valneva will only advance if private/public funding materializes. Regulatory pathway and market opportunity remain uncertain.

us.patientsK.note: CDC: ~5M global potential doses/yr travel + endemic (shigella pediatric LMIC, Zika outbreak response).

eu.patientsK.note: EMA/ECDC: travel clinic + outbreak response; shigella pediatric focus for LMIC via Gavi; Zika stockpile.

row.patientsK.note: WHO shigella LMIC pediatric ~100M birth cohort Gavi-eligible; Zika endemic Americas/APAC.

us.wtpPct.note: ~25% -- shigella primarily LMIC pediatric; US travel niche; Zika outbreak-response procurement.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~20% -- travel clinic + outbreak stockpile; EMA approval pathways open for both.

row.wtpPct.note: ~35% -- Gavi procurement for LMIC pediatric shigella; Zika PAHO stockpile LatAm.

us.priceK.note: Shigella/Zika vaccines modeled ~$0.15K per dose US travel; outbreak response procurement.

eu.priceK.note: ~$0.1K per dose travel clinic; lower via tender.

row.priceK.note: ~$0.02K per dose Gavi tiered (shigella LMIC); Zika outbreak stockpile similar.

penPct.note: ~20% peak -- early-stage assets; shigella strong LMIC volume + Gavi procurement potential.

us.reachPct.note: VLA1601 Zika vaccine; Ph1 2024, BARDA-funded; endemic resurgence risk; ~2M US travelers to endemic; launch 2029+ ~10% reach pending outbreak.

us.priceK.note: ~$0.22K per dose projected; travel vaccine class anchor.

us.wtpPct.note: ~45% -- BARDA stockpile contract baseline; ACIP travel rec contingent on outbreak resurgence.

eu.reachPct.note: ~8% EU5; EMA Zika vaccine path unclear absent outbreak.

row.reachPct.note: ~20% ROW; Brazil + Latin America endemic; PAHO + GAVI channels.

market.notes: IXIARO (Japanese encephalitis) + DUKORAL (cholera/ETEC). €157.9M total product sales 2025. 2026 guided €145-160M product sales. New CSL Seqirus distribution deal in Germany from Jan 2026. Third-party sales winding down to <5% by 2027.

us.reachPct.note: IXIARO (Japanese encephalitis) + DUKORAL (travelers diarrhea/cholera) travel franchise ~$150M/yr; military + traveler channel; stable 30% US travel reach.

us.priceK.note: IXIARO $325/dose (2-dose series), DUKORAL $80/dose; DoD negotiated discounts.

us.wtpPct.note: ~60% -- DoD contracts IXIARO military; travel clinic + ACIP travel rec for JE; commercial travel insurance.

eu.reachPct.note: ~35% EU5 travel market; EMA-approved both; UK NHS travel vaccines private + NHS endemic risk.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~55% -- EU travel vaccine established channels; NHS/SHA travel clinics.

row.reachPct.note: ~25% ROW; Japan PMDA IXIARO (JE endemic); Canada IXIARO/DUKORAL TMVN channel.

row.wtpPct.note: ~40% -- Japan NIP JE, Canada travel clinic.

us.patientsK.note: CDC: ~30M US international travelers/yr needing travel vaccines (YF, JE, typhoid, rabies, cholera).

eu.patientsK.note: ECDC: ~80M EU-5 international travelers/yr; Sanofi/GSK/Valneva/Bavarian Nordic dominant portfolio.

row.patientsK.note: WHO: ~200M global travel vaccine doses/yr; pilgrimage (Hajj) + business travel + expatriate.

us.wtpPct.note: ~55% -- out-of-pocket at travel clinics; employer-paid for business; not in ACIP routine schedule.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~50% -- national travel clinics (UK/France/Germany) + private; military/expatriate coverage.

row.wtpPct.note: ~40% -- pilgrimage mandatory (Saudi YF/meningitis); business travel private; expat employer.

us.priceK.note: YF Stamaril ~$0.25K, JE Ixiaro ~$0.3K x2, typhoid Typhim Vi ~$0.1K, rabies ~$0.3K x3 per dose.

eu.priceK.note: ~20-30% lower than US; national tenders for military/govt travel further discounted.

row.priceK.note: Pilgrimage volume driven; tiered pricing via WHO prequalification + PAHO.

penPct.note: ~25% peak of travelers actually vaccinated; steady mature market, innovation via new indications.

mega_bear.narrative: Lyme rejected + Pfizer walks away + travel vaccines commoditized
events: ✗ Pfizer/Valneva Lyme vaccine rejected by FDA — efficacy CI too wide
✗ Pfizer terminates partnership — no royalty stream
✗ Shigella Ph2 fails — pipeline empty
→ Legacy IXIARO/DUKORAL declining, company becomes sub-€1 stock

bear.narrative: Lyme rejected + IXCHIQ dead + pipeline stalls
events: ✗ FDA rejects Lyme BLA — wide CI (15.8-93.5), requires additional efficacy trial
✗ IXCHIQ permanently withdrawn globally after further SAEs
✗ S4V2 Phase 2 CHIM study disappoints on efficacy
→ Travel vaccine company only, ~€155M rev, terminal multiple

base.narrative: Lyme approved with label nuance + IXCHIQ ex-US niche + S4V2 progresses
events: ✓ Pfizer files BLA Q3 2026 — FDA accepts despite wide CI
~ Lyme approved mid-2027 with post-market commitment or narrower label
~ IXCHIQ maintains ex-US sales (€15-25M/yr) via outbreak response
✓ S4V2 Phase 2 data positive mid-2026 → Valneva takes over development

bull.narrative: Lyme blockbuster + IXCHIQ ex-US growth + Shigella first-in-class
events: ✓ Lyme approved — first vaccine in 30 years, Pfizer launches globally 2028
✓ IXCHIQ ex-US reinstatement path or 2nd-gen candidate initiated
✓ S4V2 strong Phase 2 CHIM → fast-tracked Phase 3 as first-in-class
✓ Platform validated: 4th vaccine developed, partnership pipeline expanding

psychedelic_bull.narrative: Lyme first-ever vaccine + Shigella first-in-class + Pfizer deepens partnership
events: ✓ Lyme vaccine approved — $1B+ peak sales, Valneva gets 19% royalty
✓ Shigella Phase 2 positive — first-in-class, WHO interest
✓ IXCHIQ reinstated in new ex-US markets (India, SEA)
✓ Pfizer expands partnership or acquires Valneva outright

risks: Lyme CI too wide (15.8-93.5) — FDA may require additional efficacy trial before approval
IXCHIQ US permanently gone; ex-US at risk if further SAEs emerge globally
Lyme royalty stream depends entirely on Pfizer commercial execution + willingness to file
S4V2 still Phase 2 — no efficacy data yet; human challenge model may not predict field efficacy
Cash €109.7M (end 2025) — negative operating cash flow, €500M debt facility provides buffer
Travel vaccine market cyclical and exposed to geopolitical disruption
Pfizer could renegotiate Lyme terms or deprioritize launch
Bavarian Nordic's Vimkunya (VLP chikungunya) now competes for ex-US market

lastData.title: VALOR Phase 3 — Lyme vaccine VLA15 (with Pfizer)
points: 73.2% efficacy from 28 days post-dose 4 vs placebo (95% CI 15.8-93.5)
First prespecified analysis: stat criterion NOT met (CI lower bound <20% threshold)
Second prespecified analysis: MET statistical benchmark
Fewer Lyme cases than anticipated → study underpowered
Well tolerated, no safety concerns identified
Stock crashed 38% on the data (€4.56 → €2.78) despite positive efficacy
Pfizer 'confident in the vaccine's potential' and planning regulatory submissions

nextCatalyst.title: Pfizer files BLA (FDA) + MAA (EMA) for Lyme vaccine
thresholds: Pfizer confirmed planning to file in 2026 despite wide CI. BLA prep typically takes 4-6 months post-data. Filing acceptance is not guaranteed given statistical nuance.
Two trials: Phase 2 infant safety/immunogenicity + Phase 2b CHIM (human challenge model) sponsored by LimmaTech. If both positive → Valneva takes over all further development.
Standard 12-month BLA review. Could get Priority Review (6 months) given first-in-class + unmet need. This is THE value inflection. $143M regulatory milestones at stake.

components: Commercial (IXIARO + DUKORAL): steady-state revenue × EV/Revenue multiple. No discount rate — this is current recurring revenue.
Pipeline rNPV: risk-adjusted peak revenue × peak sales multiple, discounted at pipeline DR. Covers Lyme royalties, IXCHIQ ex-US, S4V2, Zika.
Pfizer milestones: up to €225M total, risk-adjusted by Lyme regulatory approval composite PoS.
methodology.formula: EV = (CommercialRev × CommercialMult) + (Σ PipelineRA × PipelineMult / (1+DR)^n) + (Milestones × LymeComposite) + Cash. TP = EV / 174M shares × (1-Dilution).
methodology.keyDebate: Lyme regulatory approval probability is the single biggest swing factor. 73% efficacy is clinically meaningful but the CI lower bound (15.8%) in the first analysis didn't meet the ≥20% threshold. The second analysis did meet it. Whether FDA accepts this is a genuine coin-flip debate.
methodology.note: The generic renderer needs updating to support SOTP (separate commercial and pipeline valuation). Until then, this JSON serves as the data source for the standalone valneva_model.html.

resolved.notes: VALOR efficacy 73.2% (95% CI 15.8-93.5) from 28 days post-dose 4. First prespecified analysis MISSED statistical criterion (CI lower bound below 20% threshold). Second prespecified analysis MET benchmark. Trial underpowered (fewer Lyme cases than anticipated). No safety concerns. Pfizer proceeding with BLA/MAA filings Q3 2026. Stock crashed 38% despite positive point estimate.

catalysts.title: VLA15 Lyme vaccine VALOR Phase 3 readout
catalysts._rationale: Pfizer executing field efficacy study, vaccinations complete, data expected first half 2026
